findings.description: The PDF has a multi-column table layout with clear column headers and aligned data. The HTML converts this to a simplified table structure where the Prefix column and City/County column are merged/stacked, losing the distinct columnar presentation.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Prefix' and 'City/County' as separate, clearly aligned columns. HTML shows them stacked (e.g., 'County of' on one line, 'Alamance' below it) within the same cell.

findings.description: The HTML table loses the dollar sign ($) prefix formatting on many currency columns and converts dash/empty values inconsistently. The 'Sales Tax on Piped Natural Gas' column values that are shown as '-' in the PDF are rendered as '$ -' in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows clean '$' with amounts or '-' for empty. HTML shows '$ -' or '$·' for empty values.

findings.description: The header section formatting differs - the PDF has a formal title block with 'Utilities Sales Distribution', distribution report header, date range, and distribution date clearly formatted. The HTML presents these as plain text without the same visual hierarchy.
findings.evidence: PDF has underlined title 'Utilities Sales Distribution' and bold headers. HTML shows plain text headers.

findings.description: Currency values in HTML sometimes show slight formatting differences but the actual numerical data appears to match across all pages.
findings.evidence: Values like $47,895.38 in PDF match $47,895.38 in HTML for Alamance County.

findings.description: Same layout issue persists on all subsequent pages - the Prefix and City/County columns are combined in the HTML rendering, changing the table from 6 effective columns to a different visual structure.
findings.evidence: All pages show 'Town of' and city name stacked vertically in HTML vs side-by-side in PDF columns.

findings.description: Reading order is preserved - data flows in the same city/county sequence from top to bottom in both PDF and HTML across all pages.
findings.evidence: Page 2 starts with Elizabethtown in both PDF and HTML, followed by the same sequence of cities.

findings.description: No heading hierarchy exists in the HTML. The document has no h1, h2, or any heading tags. The title and report header information are placed in a div with class 'page-header' as plain paragraphs rather than proper heading elements.
findings.evidence:
The PDF title 'Utilities Sales Distribution / Gas, Power, Telecommunications, and Video Programming' and 'Distribution Report for Qtr 10/01/2017 - 12/31/2017' are rendered as

tags inside a

instead of heading elements.

findings.description: Page footer elements are repeated 11 times at the bottom of the HTML despite all content being in a single table. These footers from individual PDF pages are redundant in the consolidated HTML format.
findings.evidence: 11 separate <div class='page-footer'> elements appear at the bottom: 'Page 1 of 11' through 'Page 11 of 11'

findings.description: Spot-checked numerous rows across all 11 pages and data values match the PDF. The total row matches: $3,214,158.82 / $71,087,034.03 / $11,491,006.01 / $18,466,503.41 / $104,258,702.27. Row count appears complete.
findings.evidence: TOTALS row and individual entries like City of Charlotte ($483,190.23 / $9,456,570.76 / $1,698,855.61 / $1,871,907.08 / $13,510,523.68) match PDF.
